title: Mia's Lost Cat
text:
On Monday morning, Mia could not find her cat, Bella. Bella always slept on the warm kitchen floor, but the floor was empty. Mia looked under the bed and behind the sofa. She called Bella's name many times, but the cat did not come.

Mia felt worried. She put on her shoes and went outside. The garden was quiet. Then she heard a soft sound from the tall tree near the fence. She looked up and saw Bella sitting on a high branch. The cat looked scared.

Mia asked her neighbor, Mr. Park, for help. He brought a long ladder and climbed up slowly. Soon Bella was safe in Mia's arms. Mia said thank you many times. After that day, she always kept the kitchen window closed at night.

title: A Rainy Day Plan
text:
Tom wanted to play soccer in the park on Saturday. He woke up early and looked out of the window. The sky was gray, and rain was falling hard. Tom felt sad because he could not go outside.

His mother saw his face and smiled. "We can have fun inside too," she said. She found some old boxes in the closet. Together they made a small house from the boxes. Tom's little sister, Lucy, painted the walls with bright colors.

They ate lunch inside their box house and told funny stories. The rain did not stop all day, but Tom did not mind anymore. In the evening, the family watched a movie together. Tom said it was one of the best Saturdays he could remember.

A boy wins an important soccer game

title: The New Student
text:
A new student came to our class on Tuesday. His name was Daniel, and he was from another city. He sat alone at the back and did not talk to anyone. At lunch, he ate by himself near the window.

I watched him for a while. He looked nervous, and he did not smile. I remembered my first day at this school. I felt the same way back then. So I walked over and asked, "Can I sit with you?"

Daniel looked surprised, but then he smiled. We talked about football and games. He liked the same team as me! After lunch, I showed him the library and the music room.

Now Daniel is one of my best friends. Sometimes a small question can start a big friendship.

title: Grandpa's Little Garden
text:
My grandpa has a small garden behind his house. It is not very big, but he loves it very much. Every morning, he goes out with a cup of tea and looks at his plants. He grows tomatoes, beans, and bright yellow flowers.

Last summer, I stayed with him for two weeks. He gave me a small spade and asked me to help. At first, I did not like the dirt on my hands. But grandpa was patient. He showed me how to water the plants and pull out weeds.

After a few days, I saw tiny green tomatoes growing. I felt proud because I helped them grow. We cooked dinner with the vegetables from the garden. Now I understand why grandpa loves his little garden so much.

title: The Slow Journey of Honey
text: Honey is one of the few foods that almost never goes bad. Jars of honey found in ancient tombs were still safe to eat after thousands of years. The secret lies in how bees make it. When a bee collects nectar from flowers, the liquid is thin and full of water. Back at the hive, bees beat their wings to dry the nectar until very little water is left. They also add a special substance from their bodies that fights harmful germs. Because honey holds so little water and is slightly acidic, bacteria cannot grow inside it. This is why honey can sit on a shelf for years without spoiling. However, honey only stays good if it is kept sealed. If a jar is left open in a damp room, the honey slowly pulls water from the air. Once enough water enters, germs can finally survive, and the honey may spoil. So the long life of honey is not magic. It is the result of careful work by bees and the simple science of dryness.

title: The City That Moved Its River
text: In the 1800s, the city of Chicago had a serious problem. The Chicago River flowed into Lake Michigan, but the lake was also where the city took its drinking water. As the city grew, dirty water from streets and factories ran into the river and then into the lake. Many people became sick, and thousands died from diseases carried by the unclean water. City leaders searched for a bold solution. Instead of cleaning the river, engineers decided to make it flow the other way. Over several years, workers dug a huge canal and changed the land around the river. By 1900, the river no longer emptied into the lake. Instead, it flowed away from the city, carrying waste toward distant rivers. The drinking water grew much cleaner, and fewer people fell ill. The project was one of the largest engineering efforts of its time. It showed that people were willing to reshape nature itself to protect public health. Even today, the Chicago River still runs in the direction that engineers chose more than a century ago.

title: How Plants Talk Underground
text: For a long time, people thought of plants as silent and alone. Each tree or flower seemed to grow on its own, fighting for sunlight and water. But scientists have learned that many plants are connected in a hidden way. Under the soil, thin threads of fungus stretch between the roots of different plants. These threads form a kind of network that links one plant to another, sometimes across a whole forest. Through this network, plants can share things. A large tree may send sugar to a smaller, shaded tree that cannot make enough food. When one plant is attacked by insects, it can send a warning signal through the threads. Nearby plants then begin to defend themselves before the insects arrive. The fungus is not helping for free. In return for moving food and signals, it takes some sugar from the plants it connects. So the relationship helps both sides. This discovery has changed the way scientists see forests. A forest is not just a crowd of separate trees. It is more like a living community that quietly works together.

title: The First Long-Distance Runners
text: Humans are not the fastest animals. A deer or a horse can easily outrun a person over a short distance. Yet in one surprising way, humans are among the best runners in the animal world. We are excellent at running long distances without stopping. Scientists believe this skill helped our ancestors survive. Long ago, some hunters used a method called persistence hunting. They would chase an animal slowly but steadily for hours under the hot sun. The animal could sprint away again and again, but it could not cool itself well. Most animals release heat by panting, which does not work while running hard. Humans, however, cool down by sweating across the whole body, even while moving. Over time, the chased animal grew too hot and tired to continue. Then the hunters could catch it. Our bodies still show signs of this ancient skill. We have long legs, springy feet, and millions of sweat glands. So the next time you feel sweat on a long walk, remember that it once helped keep your ancestors alive.

options: It made hunters run faster than the animal

questions.explanation: 동물은 헐떡여서 열을 식히는데 달리면서는 잘 안 되고, 인간은 움직이면서도 땀으로 식힌다고 했으므로 1번이 추론으로 맞다.

questions.q: Why could the hunted animal not escape forever, even though it ran faster?
options: It became too hot and tired to keep going
It forgot which direction to run

questions.explanation: 본문은 동물이 계속 질주해도 몸을 잘 식히지 못해 너무 더워지고 지쳐서 멈췄다고 했으므로 0번이 맞다.

questions.q: How do most animals release heat, according to the passage?
options: By sweating across the body
By panting
By drinking water

questions.explanation: 본문에 대부분의 동물은 헐떡임(panting)으로 열을 내보낸다고 했으므로 1번이 정답이다.

questions.q: Which body feature is mentioned as a sign of the ancient running skill?
options: Sharp teeth
Long legs
Thick fur
Large ears

gist.q: Which statement best expresses the central idea?
options: Humans became great long-distance runners, a skill that helped early hunters.
Humans can outrun every animal over any distance.

title: Why Some Birds Sleep With One Eye Open
text:
Sleep is supposed to be a moment of total surrender, when the brain shuts out the world and recovers. Yet for many animals, switching off completely would be a fatal luxury. A duck floating at the edge of a flock cannot afford to ignore the approach of a fox, and a dolphin must keep swimming to the surface to breathe. Evolution has produced a remarkable compromise: these animals can rest one half of the brain while the other half stays alert. During this state, scientists have observed that the eye linked to the resting half closes, while the eye linked to the watchful half remains open.

The arrangement is not random. Ducks positioned on the outer edge of a sleeping group spend more time in this half-and-half mode, and they reliably keep the open eye pointed away from their companions, toward the direction danger is most likely to come from. Birds in the protected center, by contrast, are far more willing to shut down both halves at once.

What this suggests is that the brain can measure risk and ration its own rest accordingly. Sleep, in other words, is not a single fixed thing but a flexible resource that an animal spends carefully, holding back just enough wakefulness to survive the night.

questions.q: What is the main point the passage makes about sleep in certain animals?
options: Sleep can be adjusted by the brain according to how much danger is present.
Animals that sleep in groups never face predators.
Birds and dolphins require far more sleep than other animals.
Total unconsciousness is the safest form of rest for all species.
questions.explanation: 마지막 문단이 핵심입니다. 잠은 '고정된 하나'가 아니라 '신중하게 쓰는 유연한 자원'이며, 뇌가 위험을 측정해 휴식을 배분한다고 말합니다. 따라서 정답은 0번입니다. 1, 2, 3번은 본문이 주장하지 않는 내용입니다.

questions.q: Why do ducks on the edge of a flock keep their open eye pointed outward?
options: Because the inner birds block their view of each other.
Because the greatest threat is more likely to come from outside the group.
Because the light is brighter on the outside of the flock.
Because they are trying to signal to the birds in the center.
questions.explanation: 본문은 가장자리 오리가 뜬 눈을 '위험이 올 가능성이 가장 큰 방향'으로 향한다고 명시합니다. 무리 바깥이 위협 방향이라는 추론이 가능하므로 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What can be inferred about birds sleeping in the center of a group?
options: They are more likely to be attacked than edge birds.
They sleep less deeply because of the noise.
They feel safe enough to rest both halves of the brain at once.
They take turns staying awake to guard the others.
questions.explanation: 본문은 중앙의 새들이 '양쪽 반구를 동시에 끄는 것을 훨씬 더 기꺼이' 한다고 했습니다. 이는 더 안전하다고 느끼기 때문이라는 추론으로 이어지므로 정답은 2번입니다.

questions.q: According to the passage, why must a dolphin avoid completely switching off?
options: It needs to keep watch for sharks at all times.
It must keep swimming to the surface to breathe.
It cannot close its eyes underwater.
It hunts mainly while it is asleep.
questions.explanation: 본문은 돌고래가 '숨을 쉬기 위해 수면으로 계속 헤엄쳐야 한다'고 직접 말합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: The phrase 'a fatal luxury' suggests that for these animals, deep sleep is:
options: a pleasure they can enjoy only when young
something desirable but dangerous to indulge fully
completely impossible under any conditions
less important than finding food
questions.explanation: '치명적인 사치'라는 표현은 깊은 잠이 누리고 싶지만 완전히 탐닉하면 위험하다는 뜻입니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

gist.q: Which sentence best captures the central idea of the passage?
options: The brain can ration sleep based on the level of surrounding danger.
Ducks and dolphins are the only animals that sleep at all.
Sleeping in a group is always safer than sleeping alone.
Predators rarely attack animals while they are resting.

scan.q: What state allows these animals to rest one half of the brain while the other stays alert, as named in the text?

title: The Hidden Cost of a Perfect Lawn
text:
A smooth, uniform green lawn has long been treated as a sign of a well-kept home. Yet ecologists increasingly argue that this familiar image carries a hidden environmental cost. To stay flawless, a typical lawn demands frequent watering, regular mowing, and a steady supply of fertilizers and weed killers. Each of these inputs consumes resources, and some leave troubling traces behind.

The problem begins with what a lawn is not. A single species of trimmed grass offers almost nothing to the insects, birds, and small mammals that depend on a varied landscape. Bees and butterflies, in particular, find little food on a surface from which every flowering 'weed' has been removed. In this sense, the tidier the lawn looks, the emptier it tends to be.

There are also less visible effects. Chemicals applied to keep grass uniform can run off into streams after rain, while gas-powered mowers add to local air pollution. None of this means lawns must vanish. A growing number of homeowners are simply rethinking the standard, allowing clover and wildflowers to return, mowing less often, and accepting a little untidiness. The reward is a yard that hums with life rather than one that merely looks orderly, and the surprising lesson is that what appears neglected may in fact be far more alive.

questions.q: What is the central argument of the passage?
options: Lawns should be replaced entirely with concrete to save water.
A perfectly neat lawn comes with environmental costs that a less tidy yard can avoid.
Bees and butterflies are the only species harmed by mowing.
Fertilizers are harmless as long as they are applied in small amounts.
questions.explanation: 본문 전체는 완벽하게 깔끔한 잔디가 자원 소모와 생태적 빈곤이라는 숨은 비용을 가지며, 덜 정돈된 마당이 이를 피할 수 있다고 주장합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: Why does the passage say that 'the tidier the lawn looks, the emptier it tends to be'?
options: Because neat lawns are usually located in empty neighborhoods.
Because a uniform, weed-free surface offers little food or habitat for wildlife.
Because tidy lawns are watered so much that the soil washes away.
Because homeowners with neat lawns spend less time outdoors.
questions.explanation: 본문은 다듬어진 단일 종 잔디가 곤충·새·작은 포유류에게 거의 아무것도 제공하지 않고, 꽃 피는 '잡초'가 제거되면 벌과 나비가 먹이를 찾기 어렵다고 합니다. 깔끔할수록 생물이 적다는 뜻이므로 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What does the passage imply by saying 'what appears neglected may in fact be far more alive'?
options: Untidy yards are a sign that the owner has given up on gardening.
A yard that looks unkempt can support more living things than a perfect one.
Wild plants will eventually kill all the grass in a lawn.
Animals prefer to live only in places humans never visit.
questions.explanation: 본문은 클로버와 야생화를 되돌리고 덜 깎으면 생명으로 북적이는 마당이 된다고 했습니다. 방치된 듯 보여도 실제로는 더 살아있을 수 있다는 의미이므로 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: According to the passage, what can happen to lawn chemicals after rain?
options: They evaporate harmlessly into the air.
They run off into streams.
They make the grass grow taller overnight.
They turn the soil a darker color.
questions.explanation: 본문은 잔디를 균일하게 유지하려 뿌린 화학물질이 비가 온 뒤 '하천으로 흘러들 수 있다'고 명시합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What is the author's attitude toward lawns overall?
options: Lawns must be completely eliminated.
Lawns are fine as long as they look perfectly neat.
Lawns can stay, but the usual standard of perfection should be reconsidered.
Lawns are the single greatest environmental threat today.
questions.explanation: 본문은 '잔디가 사라져야 한다는 뜻은 아니다'라고 하면서 기준을 다시 생각하자고 제안합니다. 따라서 정답은 2번입니다.

gist.q: Which statement best summarizes the passage?
options: Maintaining a flawless lawn has ecological costs, and a less tidy yard supports more life.
Mowing a lawn is the best way to attract bees and butterflies.
Watering grass frequently is the key to a healthy garden.
Wildflowers are dangerous weeds that should always be removed.

scan.q: Which two flowering plants does the passage mention homeowners allowing to return?

title: How the Tongue Maps Sour and Bitter
text:
Many people grow up believing that the tongue is divided into fixed zones, with sweetness sensed at the tip and bitterness at the back. This neat map appears in countless textbooks, yet it is largely a myth born from a mistranslation of early research. In reality, taste receptors for all the basic flavors are scattered across the whole surface of the tongue, though some regions are slightly more sensitive than others.

What the old map got wrong about location, it also got wrong about purpose. Taste is not simply a guide to pleasure; it is an ancient warning system. Bitterness, in particular, often signals the presence of compounds that could be toxic, which may explain why children reject bitter vegetables so strongly. Sourness can warn that a fruit is unripe or that food has begun to spoil. These reactions are not mere preferences but inherited defenses.

Interestingly, culture can override biology. Adults around the world learn to enjoy coffee, dark chocolate, and sharp cheeses, flavors that a young child would spit out. This shift reveals something important about human taste: it begins as a set of instinctive alarms, but experience gradually teaches us which alarms we can safely ignore, turning early warnings into acquired pleasures.

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: The tongue-zone map is correct but rarely taught in schools.
Taste is spread across the tongue and works as a warning system that culture can later reshape.
Children and adults have identical taste preferences.
Bitter foods are always poisonous and should be avoided.
questions.explanation: 본문은 미각 수용체가 혀 전체에 퍼져 있다는 점, 미각이 경고 체계라는 점, 그리고 문화/경험이 그 경고를 재구성한다는 점을 통합해 다룹니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: Why might children reject bitter vegetables so strongly, according to the passage?
options: Because their tongues lack receptors for bitterness.
Because bitterness can signal potentially toxic compounds, triggering an inherited defense.
Because vegetables are usually served cold.
Because they have not yet learned to chew properly.
questions.explanation: 본문은 쓴맛이 독성 화합물의 존재를 알리는 경우가 많고, 이것이 아이들이 쓴 채소를 강하게 거부하는 이유일 수 있다고 설명합니다. 물려받은 방어라는 표현과도 연결되므로 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What does the passage suggest about adults who enjoy coffee and dark chocolate?
options: They have lost the ability to taste bitterness.
They have learned through experience that certain bitter warnings can be safely ignored.
They were born with different taste receptors than children.
They no longer rely on taste to detect spoiled food.
questions.explanation: 본문은 경험이 어떤 경보를 안전하게 무시할 수 있는지 가르쳐, 초기 경고를 후천적 즐거움으로 바꾼다고 말합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: According to the passage, where did the myth of the tongue map come from?
options: A famous chef's cookbook.
A mistranslation of early research.
A children's science television show.
A survey of adult coffee drinkers.
questions.explanation: 본문은 그 깔끔한 지도가 '초기 연구의 오역에서 비롯된 신화'라고 직접 말합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What does sourness warn about, based on the passage?
options: That a food is too salty.
That a fruit may be unripe or food may have begun to spoil.
That a dish contains too much sugar.
That a meal is dangerously hot.
questions.explanation: 본문은 신맛이 과일이 덜 익었거나 음식이 상하기 시작했음을 경고할 수 있다고 명시합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

gist.q: Which sentence best captures the passage's overall message?
options: Human taste starts as a system of instinctive warnings that experience can turn into pleasures.
The tongue map accurately shows where each flavor is sensed.
People are born already loving bitter and sour foods.
Taste exists only to make eating enjoyable.

scan.q: What does the passage call the kind of system that bitterness and sourness belong to?

title: The Library That Lends Out Tools and Skills
text:
In a growing number of towns, the local library no longer lends only books. Visitors can now borrow a power drill, a sewing machine, a telescope, or even a metal detector, taking it home for a week the way they might take a novel. These so-called 'libraries of things' rest on a simple observation: most people use such items rarely, yet still pay to own, store, and maintain them. Sharing them spreads the cost across an entire community.

The appeal goes beyond saving money. When a tool is borrowed rather than bought, fewer copies need to be manufactured, which reduces waste and the demand for raw materials. A single shared drill may replace dozens of private ones gathering dust in closets. For many supporters, this quiet shift represents a more thoughtful relationship with the objects in our lives.

There is a social dimension as well. Borrowing a ladder or a pasta maker often means a short conversation with a librarian or a neighbor who has used it before. Some lending programs add workshops where members teach one another how to repair a bicycle or bake bread. In this way, the library becomes less a warehouse of goods than a meeting point, where what circulates most freely is not the tools themselves but the knowledge of how to use them.

questions.q: What is the main idea of the passage?
options: Libraries should stop lending books and focus only on tools.
Tool-lending libraries save money, reduce waste, and build community knowledge.
Power drills are the most commonly borrowed item in modern libraries.
Most people prefer to buy their own tools rather than share them.
questions.explanation: 본문은 '물건 도서관'이 비용 절감, 폐기물 감소, 그리고 지식·공동체 형성이라는 세 측면을 함께 다룹니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: Why does sharing a drill reduce demand for raw materials?
options: Because borrowed drills are made from recycled metal only.
Because one shared drill can replace many privately owned ones, so fewer need to be made.
Because libraries repair drills instead of buying new parts.
Because people stop using drills once they borrow one.
questions.explanation: 본문은 하나의 공유 드릴이 옷장에서 먼지만 쌓이는 수십 개의 개인 드릴을 대체할 수 있고, 그만큼 적게 제조되어 폐기물과 원료 수요가 준다고 설명합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What does the final sentence suggest is the most valuable thing the library circulates?
options: The tools that members borrow.
The fees collected from members.
The knowledge of how to use the tools.
The space the library occupies.
questions.explanation: 마지막 문장은 가장 자유롭게 순환하는 것이 '도구 자체가 아니라 그것을 쓰는 방법에 대한 지식'이라고 말합니다. 따라서 정답은 2번입니다.

questions.q: According to the passage, what observation are 'libraries of things' based on?
options: That books are no longer popular with readers.
That most people use such items rarely yet still pay to own and store them.
That tools break down faster when shared.
That neighbors rarely talk to one another.
questions.explanation: 본문은 이 개념이 '대부분의 사람들이 그런 물건을 드물게 쓰면서도 소유·보관·유지에 돈을 낸다'는 관찰에 기반한다고 명시합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

questions.q: What can be inferred about the author's view of these lending programs?
options: They are an impractical idea that wastes public money.
They offer benefits that go beyond simply saving individuals money.
They will soon replace all traditional book libraries.
They mainly benefit people who already own many tools.
questions.explanation: 본문은 '매력은 돈을 아끼는 것을 넘어선다'고 하며 환경적·사회적 이점을 긍정적으로 서술합니다. 따라서 정답은 1번입니다.

gist.q: Which statement best summarizes the passage?
options: Tool-lending libraries share rarely used items, cutting costs and waste while spreading skills and community ties.
Modern libraries have abandoned books in favor of household gadgets.
Borrowing tools is more expensive than buying them new.
Workshops are the only reason people visit lending libraries.

scan.q: What nickname does the passage give to libraries that lend items like drills and telescopes?

title: The Hidden Cost of Undercooling
text:
When a molten metal cools, common intuition suggests it should freeze the moment its temperature drops below the melting point. In practice, liquids routinely persist as liquids well below this threshold, a condition metallurgists call undercooling. The reason lies in the energetics of forming the first stable solid cluster. Creating a new solid region generates an interface between solid and liquid, and that interface carries an energy penalty. For very small clusters, the surface penalty outweighs the energy released by solidification, so the embryonic crystal dissolves before it can grow. Only once a cluster exceeds a critical radius does further growth lower the system's total energy, allowing the solid to expand spontaneously.

The degree of undercooling a melt can sustain is therefore not a fixed property but depends heavily on circumstance. A perfectly clean melt, free of foreign particles and container walls, may cool dozens or even hundreds of degrees below its melting point before solidifying. Introduce a rough surface or a stray oxide particle, however, and crystals nucleate almost immediately, because such surfaces reduce the energy needed to form a viable cluster. This is why foundries deliberately add inoculant particles: by supplying abundant nucleation sites, they trigger solidification at modest undercooling and produce many small grains rather than a few coarse ones. The resulting fine-grained microstructure tends to be stronger and more uniform. Undercooling, then, is not merely a curiosity of phase change but a lever that engineers pull to govern the internal architecture of a casting before it has even finished freezing.

questions.q: Which statement best captures the central argument of the passage?
options: Undercooling is an unavoidable defect that weakens all metal castings.
The extent of undercooling depends on conditions and can be exploited to control a casting's microstructure.
Molten metals always freeze precisely at their melting point under industrial conditions.
Surface energy is irrelevant once a melt drops below its melting temperature.
questions.explanation: 지문 전체는 과냉이 고정된 성질이 아니라 상황(불순물, 접종제 등)에 따라 달라지며, 엔지니어가 이를 활용해 주조물 내부 구조를 제어한다고 말한다. 마지막 문장이 이 논지를 직접 요약한다.

questions.q: A foundry wants a casting with many fine grains. Based on the passage, it should most likely:
options: Use an exceptionally clean melt free of any foreign particles.
Cool the melt as slowly as possible to avoid undercooling.
Add inoculant particles to provide abundant nucleation sites.
Raise the melting point of the alloy before pouring.
questions.explanation: 지문은 접종제 입자가 핵생성 자리를 풍부하게 공급해 작은 결정립을 많이 만든다고 설명한다. 반면 깨끗한 용융물은 큰 과냉을 견뎌 거친 결정립을 만들기 쉬우므로 정반대다.

questions.q: Why does an embryonic crystal below the critical radius dissolve rather than grow?
options: Its temperature is still above the melting point.
The interface energy penalty exceeds the energy released by solidifying.
Foreign particles physically break it apart.
The melt has not yet reached the inoculant threshold.
questions.explanation: 지문은 작은 클러스터에서는 계면이 주는 에너지 손실(표면 페널티)이 응고로 방출되는 에너지를 능가해 결정이 다시 녹는다고 명시한다.

questions.q: According to the passage, why does a rough surface or oxide particle trigger nucleation almost immediately?
options: It raises the melt's overall temperature.
It increases the critical radius required for growth.
It reduces the energy needed to form a viable cluster.
It removes the latent heat of solidification.
questions.explanation: 지문은 그런 표면이 '생존 가능한 클러스터를 형성하는 데 필요한 에너지를 줄여준다'고 직접 설명한다.

questions.q: What can be inferred about a melt that solidifies only after cooling hundreds of degrees below its melting point?
options: It was probably very clean and free of nucleation sites.
It must have contained many inoculant particles.
It will necessarily form a fine-grained structure.
It violates the energetics described in the passage.
questions.explanation: 지문은 이물질과 용기 벽이 없는 깨끗한 용융물이 수십~수백 도 과냉을 견딜 수 있다고 했으므로, 큰 과냉을 보인 용융물은 핵생성 자리가 없는 깨끗한 상태였다고 추론된다.

gist.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How engineers use the controllable nature of undercooling to shape a casting's microstructure.
Why all metals freeze exactly at their melting point.
The chemical composition of common foundry inoculants.
A history of metal casting techniques.

scan.q: What term do metallurgists use for a liquid persisting below its melting point?

title: Stress Locked in Layer by Layer
text:
Metal additive manufacturing builds a part one thin layer at a time, fusing powder with a focused laser or electron beam. This layer-by-layer logic gives designers extraordinary geometric freedom, but it also embeds a quieter problem: residual stress. Each pass of the beam melts a narrow track of metal that then cools and contracts within seconds. Because the freshly solidified material is anchored to the cooler, already-solid layers beneath it, it cannot contract freely. The constraint pulls the new layer into tension while pushing the underlying material into compression. Repeated thousands of times, these locked-in stresses accumulate throughout the build.

The consequences are not merely theoretical. A part may warp as it is released from the build plate, or a crack may propagate through a region where tensile stress exceeds the material's strength. In severe cases, distortion during the build itself causes a partially finished layer to curl upward and collide with the powder-spreading mechanism, halting the job entirely. Engineers counter these effects with several strategies. Preheating the build plate narrows the temperature gap between new and old material, reducing the contraction mismatch. Carefully ordered scan patterns distribute heat more evenly rather than concentrating it. After the build, a heat treatment called stress relief allows the metal to relax internally without changing its shape appreciably. None of these measures eliminates residual stress outright; they manage it. Understanding where and why stress concentrates has thus become as central to additive manufacturing as the geometry of the part itself, shaping decisions long before the first layer is ever deposited.

questions.q: What is the main point the passage makes about residual stress in additive manufacturing?
options: It is a minor side effect that engineers can safely ignore.
It arises from the layer-by-layer process and must be actively managed rather than eliminated.
It can be completely removed by preheating the build plate.
It only matters for parts with complex geometries.
questions.explanation: 지문은 잔류응력이 층별 적층 방식에서 필연적으로 발생하며, 여러 전략으로 '제거'가 아니라 '관리'된다고 결론짓는다.

questions.q: Why does preheating the build plate help reduce residual stress?
options: It melts the powder more completely so no contraction occurs.
It narrows the temperature gap between new and old material, lessening the contraction mismatch.
It increases the tensile strength of the finished metal.
It speeds up the scan so less heat is deposited.
questions.explanation: 지문은 예열이 새 재료와 기존 재료 사이 온도 차를 좁혀 수축 불일치를 줄인다고 명시한다. 응력의 근본 원인이 수축 불일치이므로 이를 완화한다.

questions.q: Based on the passage, why can a freshly solidified track not contract freely?
options: The laser continues to heat it indefinitely.
It is anchored to the cooler, already-solid layers beneath it.
The powder around it prevents any movement.
Its melting point is lower than that of the layers below.
questions.explanation: 지문은 갓 응고된 재료가 아래의 더 차갑고 이미 굳은 층에 고정되어 자유롭게 수축할 수 없다고 설명한다.

questions.q: According to the passage, what can happen in severe cases of distortion during the build?
options: The laser automatically shuts down to prevent damage.
A partially finished layer curls upward and collides with the powder-spreading mechanism, halting the job.
The build plate melts entirely.
The part becomes stronger due to work hardening.
questions.explanation: 지문은 심한 경우 부분적으로 완성된 층이 위로 말려 분말 도포 기구와 충돌해 작업이 완전히 멈춘다고 서술한다.

questions.q: What does the final sentence imply about how residual stress affects the design process?
options: Stress is considered only after a part fails in service.
Designers must account for stress before depositing the first layer, making it as important as geometry.
Geometry is irrelevant once stress relief is applied.
Residual stress is decided entirely by the choice of powder.
questions.explanation: 마지막 문장은 응력이 집중되는 위치와 이유를 이해하는 것이 부품 형상만큼 중요해졌고, 첫 층을 깔기 훨씬 전부터 의사결정을 좌우한다고 말한다.

gist.q: Which title best reflects the passage's overall focus?
options: How layer-by-layer building creates residual stress that engineers must manage.
Why electron beams are superior to lasers in additive manufacturing.
The geometric advantages of 3D-printed metal parts.
A comparison of powder materials used in printing.

scan.q: What is the name of the post-build heat treatment that lets metal relax internally?

title: Why Fast Charging Threatens Battery Life
text:
A lithium-ion battery stores energy by shuttling lithium ions between two electrodes through a liquid electrolyte. During charging, ions leave the positive electrode and travel to the negative one, where they slip into the layered structure of graphite in a process called intercalation. Ideally each ion settles neatly between the carbon layers. When a battery is charged too quickly, however, ions arrive faster than the graphite can absorb them, and some are forced to deposit on the electrode surface as metallic lithium instead. This unwanted deposition, known as lithium plating, is the first step toward trouble.

Plated lithium does not lie flat. It grows into needle-like projections called dendrites that extend outward with each charging cycle. Two distinct hazards follow. First, some plated lithium becomes electrically isolated from the electrode, so it can no longer participate in storing charge; the battery's usable capacity quietly shrinks. Second, and more dangerously, a dendrite may grow far enough to pierce the thin separator that keeps the two electrodes apart. If it bridges them, the resulting short circuit can release energy rapidly and trigger overheating. Because plating worsens at low temperatures, where ion movement through graphite slows, manufacturers often restrict fast charging in the cold. They also design battery-management systems that taper the charging current as the cell fills, trading a few extra minutes of charging time for a longer service life. The same convenience that users prize, then, sits in direct tension with the chemistry that keeps a cell safe and durable.

questions.q: What central idea does the passage convey?
options: Fast charging is harmless as long as the battery is kept cold.
Charging too quickly causes lithium plating, which threatens both capacity and safety, so charging must be managed.
Dendrites improve a battery's energy storage over time.
Graphite electrodes should be replaced with metallic lithium for better performance.
questions.explanation: 지문은 과속 충전이 리튬 플레이팅을 유발하고 이것이 용량 감소와 안전 위험으로 이어지므로 충전을 관리해야 한다고 말한다.

questions.q: Why do manufacturers often restrict fast charging in cold conditions?
options: Cold weather drains the battery faster than warm weather.
At low temperatures ion movement through graphite slows, which worsens lithium plating.
The electrolyte freezes solid below room temperature.
Dendrites dissolve when the battery is cold.
questions.explanation: 지문은 저온에서 흑연을 통한 이온 이동이 느려져 플레이팅이 심해지므로 추울 때 급속 충전을 제한한다고 설명한다.

questions.q: Why does a battery's usable capacity 'quietly shrink' as described?
options: The electrolyte slowly evaporates during charging.
Some plated lithium becomes electrically isolated and can no longer store charge.
The graphite layers expand and crack open.
The separator absorbs lithium ions permanently.
questions.explanation: 지문은 도금된 리튬 일부가 전극에서 전기적으로 고립되어 더 이상 전하 저장에 참여하지 못해 용량이 줄어든다고 명시한다.

questions.q: According to the passage, what is the more dangerous hazard posed by dendrites?
options: They slow down the intercalation of future ions.
They may pierce the separator and create a short circuit that causes overheating.
They permanently lower the battery's voltage.
They cause the positive electrode to dissolve.
questions.explanation: 지문은 더 위험한 두 번째 위험으로, 덴드라이트가 분리막을 뚫고 두 전극을 연결해 단락과 과열을 일으킬 수 있다고 서술한다.

questions.q: What does tapering the charging current as the cell fills suggest about the manufacturers' priorities?
options: They value the fastest possible charging above all else.
They accept slightly longer charging times to extend the battery's service life.
They aim to keep the battery permanently at low temperature.
They want to maximize dendrite growth for stability.
questions.explanation: 지문은 셀이 차오를수록 충전 전류를 줄여 '몇 분의 추가 충전 시간을 더 긴 수명과 맞바꾼다'고 했으므로, 제조사가 수명을 위해 속도를 양보함을 알 수 있다.

gist.q: What is the passage primarily explaining?
options: How lithium plating from fast charging endangers a battery and why charging is therefore managed.
The chemical formula of common battery electrolytes.
Why graphite is cheaper than other electrode materials.
How to recycle worn-out lithium-ion batteries.

scan.q: What is the name of the process by which lithium ions slip between graphite's carbon layers?

title: The Tangled Properties of Thermoelectrics
text:
Thermoelectric materials convert a temperature difference directly into electrical voltage, offering the appealing prospect of harvesting electricity from waste heat with no moving parts. Their performance is captured by a single dimensionless figure of merit that rewards three things at once: a large voltage generated per degree of temperature difference, high electrical conductivity, and low thermal conductivity. The first two ensure that useful current flows, while the third preserves the temperature gradient that drives the device. A good thermoelectric must therefore conduct electricity like a metal yet conduct heat like a glass.

The difficulty is that these properties are not independent. Raising the concentration of charge carriers typically improves electrical conductivity but simultaneously lowers the voltage generated per degree, so the two pull against each other. Worse, charge carriers themselves transport heat, meaning that boosting electrical conduction often raises thermal conduction as well. Decades of research have thus focused not on improving any single property in isolation but on decoupling those that nature has bound together. One influential strategy targets the portion of heat carried by lattice vibrations rather than by charge carriers. By introducing nanoscale features and deliberate disorder, researchers scatter these vibrations and suppress that channel of heat flow while leaving electrical conduction comparatively untouched. This approach has yielded some of the largest gains in the field. The lesson is broadly instructive: progress in thermoelectrics has come less from chasing one ideal number than from learning which trade-offs can be loosened and which must, for now, be accepted.

questions.q: Which statement best expresses the passage's main argument?
options: Thermoelectric materials are useless because their properties conflict.
Progress in thermoelectrics comes from decoupling interrelated properties rather than optimizing any single one.
The figure of merit depends on only one material property.
Lattice vibrations are the sole source of electrical conduction in thermoelectrics.
questions.explanation: 지문은 열전 성능 향상이 단일 물성을 좇는 것이 아니라 자연이 묶어 놓은 상호의존적 물성들을 분리(decoupling)하는 데서 나왔다고 논증한다.

questions.q: Why is it described as difficult to improve a thermoelectric simply by raising the carrier concentration?
options: It has no effect on any property.
It improves electrical conductivity but lowers the voltage per degree, so the two work against each other.
It only changes the thermal conductivity of the lattice.
It eliminates the temperature gradient entirely.
questions.explanation: 지문은 운반자 농도를 높이면 전기전도도는 좋아지나 도(degree)당 전압이 낮아져 둘이 서로 상쇄한다고 설명한다.

questions.q: The phrase 'conduct electricity like a metal yet conduct heat like a glass' implies that an ideal thermoelectric should have:
options: High electrical conductivity and high thermal conductivity.
Low electrical conductivity and low thermal conductivity.
High electrical conductivity and low thermal conductivity.
Properties identical to those of ordinary metals.
questions.explanation: 금속은 전기를 잘 통하고 유리는 열을 잘 통하지 않으므로, 이상적 열전체는 높은 전기전도도와 낮은 열전도도를 동시에 가져야 한다는 뜻이다. 지문의 figure of merit 설명과도 일치한다.

questions.q: According to the passage, what does the influential strategy of introducing nanoscale features and disorder accomplish?
options: It raises the carrier concentration to a maximum.
It scatters lattice vibrations to suppress that channel of heat flow while leaving electrical conduction comparatively untouched.
It increases thermal conductivity to preserve current.
It removes all charge carriers from the material.
questions.explanation: 지문은 나노 구조와 의도적 무질서가 격자 진동을 산란시켜 그 경로의 열 흐름을 억제하면서 전기 전도는 상대적으로 건드리지 않는다고 서술한다.

questions.q: What broader lesson does the passage draw from thermoelectric research?
options: All material trade-offs can eventually be eliminated.
Chasing a single ideal number is the surest route to progress.
Advances depend on recognizing which trade-offs can be loosened and which must be accepted for now.
Waste heat cannot realistically be converted into electricity.
questions.explanation: 마지막 문장은 발전이 하나의 이상적 수치를 좇기보다 어떤 상충을 완화할 수 있고 어떤 것은 당분간 받아들여야 하는지를 배우는 데서 왔다고 정리한다.

gist.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: Why thermoelectric progress relies on decoupling naturally interlinked material properties.
How to manufacture glass with metallic conductivity.
The history of waste-heat recovery engines with moving parts.
A method for measuring temperature differences precisely.

scan.q: What single dimensionless quantity is used to capture a thermoelectric material's performance?

validation_note: 5문항 모두 본문 근거 명확, 정답키 정확하며 metal/glass 비유 문항도 본문 figure of merit 설명과 일치해 모호하지 않음. 전부 보존. unit_count 252→250로 미세 정정(실제 250단어).

title: Interfacial Variability in Thermoelectric Contacts
text: The persistent gap between the intrinsic figure of merit reported for bulk thermoelectric materials and the conversion efficiency realised in assembled devices is increasingly attributed not to the active legs themselves but to the metallised interfaces that join them to electrodes. Contact resistance at these junctions, though nominally a second-order term, appears to dominate device-level losses once leg dimensions are miniaturised, since the parasitic contribution scales inversely with cross-sectional area. We argue that the variability of this contribution, rather than its mean magnitude, constitutes the more consequential and under-examined obstacle. Microstructural characterisation of diffusion-bonded interfaces suggests that intermetallic phases nucleate heterogeneously, producing patches of markedly differing electrical and thermal transport whose distribution is sensitive to bonding pressure, dwell temperature, and the oxidation state of the as-received powder. The conventional practice of reporting a single areal contact resistance, averaged across a coupon, may therefore obscure tail behaviour that governs reliability under thermal cycling. Our preliminary measurements, while not yet statistically conclusive, indicate that interfaces exhibiting comparable mean resistance can differ substantially in the spatial heterogeneity of that resistance, and that the more heterogeneous specimens degrade preferentially at the hot side. This would imply that qualification protocols calibrated to mean values systematically overestimate service life. We caution, however, that the apparent correlation could partly reflect measurement artefacts introduced by the scanning probe geometry, and that disentangling intrinsic heterogeneity from instrumental convolution will require complementary tomographic data. If the effect proves robust, it suggests that efforts to raise device efficiency by incremental gains in bulk transport are misdirected relative to the larger returns available from controlling interfacial uniformity. The broader implication is that thermoelectric device design ought to be reframed as a problem of statistical interface engineering rather than of material optimisation alone.

questions.q: What central claim does the passage advance about thermoelectric device losses?
options: The variability of interfacial contact resistance, more than its average value, is the critical and neglected limiting factor
Bulk material figure of merit has been systematically overstated in the literature
Thermal cycling has no measurable effect on diffusion-bonded interfaces
Larger leg cross-sections increase the parasitic contact contribution
questions.explanation: 본문은 평균 접촉저항이 아니라 그 '변동성(variability)'이 더 중대하고 덜 연구된 장애물이라고 명시적으로 주장한다('the variability of this contribution, rather than its mean magnitude, constitutes the more consequential and under-examined obstacle'). 따라서 0번이 핵심 주장이다.

questions.q: Why does the passage suggest that current qualification protocols may overestimate service life?
options: Because they ignore bulk transport entirely
Because they rely on mean contact resistance, which masks the heterogeneous tail behaviour that drives early degradation
Because they test only the cold side of the device
Because they use tomographic data that is too coarse
questions.explanation: 본문은 평균값으로 보고하면 신뢰성을 좌우하는 '꼬리(tail) 거동'이 가려진다고 했고, 평균값에 맞춘 자격검증 프로토콜이 수명을 체계적으로 과대평가한다고 추론한다. 이질적인 시편이 hot side에서 먼저 열화하므로 평균만 보면 위험을 놓친다. 따라서 1번이 옳다.

questions.q: What caveat do the authors raise about their own preliminary correlation?
options: That the heterogeneity may be partly an artefact of the scanning probe geometry rather than wholly intrinsic
That the bonding pressure was never recorded
That the materials were not thermoelectric at all
That the mean resistance values were fabricated
questions.explanation: 본문은 관찰된 상관관계가 부분적으로 주사 탐침(scanning probe) 기하에서 비롯된 측정 인공물을 반영할 수 있다고 경고한다('could partly reflect measurement artefacts introduced by the scanning probe geometry'). 그래서 0번이 정답이다.

questions.q: What practical reorientation does the passage imply for thermoelectric design?
options: Abandoning device assembly in favour of bulk samples
Treating design as statistical interface engineering rather than material optimisation alone
Maximising leg miniaturisation regardless of contact effects
Eliminating intermetallic phases by raising dwell temperature
questions.explanation: 마지막 문장에서 본문은 열전 소자 설계를 '재료 최적화만의 문제가 아니라 통계적 계면 공학의 문제로 재구성해야 한다'고 함의한다. 따라서 1번이 옳다.

questions.q: According to the passage, how does the parasitic contact contribution scale as legs are miniaturised?
options: It scales directly with cross-sectional area
It scales inversely with cross-sectional area
It remains constant regardless of geometry
It scales with the square of dwell temperature
questions.explanation: 본문은 기생 기여가 단면적에 '반비례하여 증가(scales inversely with cross-sectional area)'한다고 명시한다. 따라서 1번이 정답이다.

gist.q: Which statement best captures the passage's overall thesis?
options: Improving bulk transport is the surest path to higher device efficiency
Controlling the statistical uniformity of metallised interfaces matters more than incremental bulk gains
Contact resistance is negligible in all thermoelectric devices
Scanning probe measurements are inherently unreliable for any material

scan.q: What does the passage say nucleates heterogeneously at diffusion-bonded interfaces?

title: Defect Stochasticity in Laser Powder-Bed Fusion
text: In laser powder-bed fusion of nickel-base superalloys, the relationship between nominal process parameters and as-built porosity remains imperfectly predictive, a discrepancy that we attribute less to incomplete physical models than to the stochastic substructure of the powder feedstock itself. Although volumetric energy density is routinely invoked as a consolidating descriptor, its explanatory power deteriorates near the boundaries of the printable window, where keyhole instabilities and lack-of-fusion defects coexist and where small fluctuations in local packing density may tip the melt pool between regimes. We contend that energy density, being a lumped scalar, cannot by construction resolve the spatially correlated variations in absorptivity that arise from satellite particles and irregular morphology. Synchrotron observations reported elsewhere have established that keyhole collapse is intermittent rather than steady; our contribution is to suggest that this intermittency is partly seeded by feedstock heterogeneity that conventional parameter optimisation treats as noise to be averaged away. If correct, this reframing implies that two builds produced under identical nominal settings, but from powder lots of differing recyclate fraction, ought to exhibit systematically distinct defect populations even when bulk density appears equivalent. Our coupon data are consistent with this expectation, though we acknowledge that the recyclate lots also differed marginally in oxygen content, a confound we could not fully isolate. The tentative conclusion is that porosity specifications expressed solely in terms of machine parameters are insufficient, and that feedstock provenance should be elevated to a first-class control variable. More speculatively, the persistence of rare large defects, which dominate fatigue life yet escape statistical sampling, suggests that qualification by mean density is structurally inadequate for fracture-critical components, irrespective of how finely the process parameters are tuned.

questions.q: What is the passage's principal argument about predicting porosity in laser powder-bed fusion?
options: Physical models are complete and require no further refinement
Feedstock heterogeneity, often treated as averageable noise, is a major and underrecognised driver of defect populations
Volumetric energy density fully captures all relevant melt-pool behaviour
Oxygen content is the sole determinant of porosity
questions.explanation: 본문은 공정 파라미터와 기공률의 불완전한 예측성을 모델의 미비보다 '분말 원료의 확률적 하부구조'에 돌리며, 종래 최적화가 평균으로 무시해온 원료 이질성이 결함 분포를 좌우한다고 주장한다. 따라서 1번이 핵심 논지다.

questions.q: Why do the authors argue that energy density is an inadequate descriptor near the printable window's boundaries?
options: Because it is impossible to measure experimentally
Because as a lumped scalar it cannot resolve spatially correlated variations in absorptivity from irregular particles
Because it only applies to titanium alloys
Because keyhole instabilities never occur there
questions.explanation: 본문은 에너지 밀도가 '집약된 스칼라(lumped scalar)'여서 위성 입자와 불규칙 형태에서 비롯되는 공간적으로 상관된 흡수율 변동을 구조적으로 분해할 수 없다고 본다. 따라서 1번이 옳다.

questions.q: What does the passage imply about two builds made under identical nominal settings but different powder lots?
options: They should be indistinguishable in every respect
They should show systematically distinct defect populations even at equivalent bulk density
They cannot be compared at all
They will always have identical fatigue life
questions.explanation: 본문은 동일한 공칭 설정이라도 재활용분 비율이 다른 분말 로트에서 만든 빌드는 벌크 밀도가 같아 보여도 '체계적으로 다른 결함 집단'을 보여야 한다고 함의한다. 따라서 1번이 정답이다.

questions.q: Why do the authors consider qualification by mean density structurally inadequate for fracture-critical parts?
options: Because mean density is impossible to compute
Because rare large defects dominate fatigue life yet evade statistical sampling
Because all defects are identical in size
Because process parameters cannot be tuned at all
questions.explanation: 본문은 피로 수명을 좌우하지만 통계 표본에서 빠져나가는 '드문 대형 결함'의 지속성 때문에 평균 밀도에 의한 자격검증이 파괴 임계 부품에 구조적으로 부적합하다고 한다. 따라서 1번이 옳다.

questions.q: Which confound do the authors admit they could not fully isolate?
options: Differences in laser wavelength
A marginal difference in oxygen content between recyclate lots

title: Limits to Grain Refinement in Rapid Solidification
text: Conventional accounts of grain refinement in rapidly solidified metals treat the achievable grain size as monotonically decreasing with cooling rate, an idealisation that becomes untenable once nucleation kinetics are confronted with finite heterogeneous site densities. We propose that a refinement limit emerges not from any thermodynamic ceiling but from the exhaustion of available nucleant particles, beyond which further increases in undercooling recruit no additional grains and instead accelerate the growth of those already established. This site-saturation regime, while qualitatively anticipated in classical treatments, has rarely been quantified for the alloy systems most relevant to additive manufacturing, where thermal gradients are steep and transient. Our modelling, predicated on a population balance over nucleation events, indicates that the plateau in attainable grain size is reached at undercoolings well below those routinely encountered in laser processing, implying that the marginal benefit of faster cooling is largely illusory once saturation is approached. We are careful to note that this inference holds only insofar as the nucleant inventory is fixed; deliberate inoculation, by replenishing the site population, could in principle defer the plateau. The practical corollary is counterintuitive: refinement strategies that pursue ever-higher cooling rates may yield diminishing returns relative to those that engineer the inoculant distribution itself. We further conjecture, though with less confidence, that the spatial clustering of nucleants, rather than merely their number density, modulates the onset of saturation, since clustered particles compete for the same depleted solute field. Should this prove correct, the homogeneity of the inoculant dispersion would matter as much as its concentration. These considerations collectively suggest that the long-standing emphasis on cooling rate as the master variable of microstructural refinement deserves qualification, and that nucleant management offers a more tractable lever for fine-grained additive builds.

title: Boundary Scattering and Lattice Thermal Conductivity
text: The suppression of lattice thermal conductivity through deliberate introduction of grain boundaries is a well-rehearsed strategy in thermoelectric optimisation, yet its efficacy is frequently overstated when the phonon spectrum is treated as monochromatic. Because heat in most chalcogenides is carried by a broad distribution of phonon mean free paths, boundary scattering preferentially attenuates the long-wavelength, long-mean-free-path carriers while leaving the short-mean-free-path population comparatively unaffected. It follows that nanostructuring yields its largest reductions in conductivity only when the grain size is commensurate with the dominant mean free paths, a condition that is alloy-specific and temperature-dependent. We caution against the prevalent inference that finer is invariably better; below a certain grain size, additional refinement scatters phonons that contribute negligibly to transport while incurring electronic penalties through carrier scattering at the same interfaces. The resulting trade-off, which couples the very quantities one wishes to decouple, may explain why reported gains in the figure of merit from nanostructuring often fall short of spectral predictions. Our analysis, drawing on a frequency-resolved accumulation function, suggests that an optimally graded distribution of grain sizes could in principle scatter across a wider band of the spectrum than any single grain size, though the synthesis of such hierarchically structured materials remains challenging and our estimates of the attainable benefit are necessarily provisional. We note further that interface chemistry, not merely interface density, may govern the phonon transmission coefficient, an effect our continuum treatment cannot capture. Taken together, these arguments imply that the rational design of low-conductivity thermoelectrics should proceed from the measured phonon spectrum of the specific compound rather than from a generic presumption that boundary proliferation is universally beneficial.

title: 网上买菜
text: 以前我妈妈每天早上都去市场买菜，她说只有自己挑的菜才新鲜。可是去年开始，她学会了用手机买菜。现在她坐在家里，点几下手机，半个小时后菜就送到家门口了。一开始我担心送来的菜不好，但是用了几次以后发现，质量其实跟市场的差不多，价格有时候还更便宜。妈妈说，最让她高兴的不是省钱，而是省下来的时间可以用来做自己喜欢的事，比如和朋友一起跳舞。

title: 养成早起的习惯
text: 我一直觉得自己不是早起的人，每天闹钟响了好几次都不想起床。后来一个朋友告诉我，如果想早起，最好的办法不是早上努力，而是晚上早点睡。我试着把睡觉的时间提前了一个小时，没想到第二天真的轻松多了。现在我每天六点起床，先去公园走二十分钟，然后回来好好吃个早饭。这样一天的开始变得安静又舒服。我才明白，习惯不是一下子养成的，而是从一个小小的改变开始的。

title: 为什么天空是蓝色的
text: 很多孩子都问过爸爸妈妈一个问题：天空为什么是蓝色的？其实答案和阳光有关。阳光看起来是白色的，但是它里面有很多种颜色。当阳光进入空气的时候，蓝色的光最容易被空气里的小东西散开，散到天空的各个地方，所以我们抬头看到的就是蓝色。到了傍晚，太阳离我们更远，阳光要走更长的路，这时候蓝色的光大部分都散没了，剩下的红色光就让天空变成了好看的橘红色。

questions.q: 白天天空看起来是蓝色的，主要是因为哪种光？
options: 红色的光
蓝色的光
白色的光
黄色的光
questions.explanation: 본문에 "蓝色的光最容易被空气里的小东西散开...所以我们抬头看到的就是蓝色"(파란빛이 가장 쉽게 흩어져 하늘이 파랗게 보인다)라고 나온다.

questions.q: 为什么傍晚的天空会变成橘红色？
options: 太阳变成了红色
蓝光大部分散没了，剩下红光
空气里没有颜色了
天空被云挡住了
questions.explanation: "蓝色的光大部分都散没了，剩下的红色光就让天空变成了...橘红色"(파란빛이 대부분 흩어져 사라지고 남은 붉은빛이 하늘을 주홍색으로 만든다)라고 설명한다.

options: 阳光只有白色一种颜色

阳光不会被空气改变
questions.explanation: 본문에 "它里面有很多种颜色"(그 안에는 여러 색이 들어있다)라고 했으므로, 햇빛은 사실 여러 색을 포함한다는 두 번째가 맞다.

questions.q: 傍晚的时候，阳光为什么要走更长的路？
options: 因为空气更厚了
因为太阳离我们更远
因为天空变小了

questions.explanation: "到了傍晚，太阳离我们更远，阳光要走更长的路"(저녁이 되면 태양이 더 멀어져 햇빛이 더 긴 길을 가야 한다)라고 직접 설명한다.

title: 一封来自老同学的信
text: 上个星期，我收到了一封信。现在大家都用手机发消息，已经很少有人写信了，所以我特别奇怪。打开一看，原来是十年没见的老同学小林写的。他在信里说，他最近搬到了一个安静的小城市，那里的生活很慢，让他想起了我们读大学的日子。他说，发消息虽然方便，但是太快了，常常说完就忘了；而写信的时候，他能慢慢想，把心里的话一个字一个字地写下来。读完信，我也决定给他回一封，而不是发消息。

title: 睡眠与记忆
text: 很多人以为睡觉只是让身体休息，其实大脑在我们睡着的时候依然非常忙碌。科学家发现，白天学到的新知识并不会马上变成长久的记忆，而是先被临时保存在大脑的某个区域。到了夜里，大脑会趁我们熟睡时把这些零散的信息重新整理，把重要的部分牢牢记住，把不重要的渐渐删掉。换句话说，睡眠就像一位认真的管理员，每天夜里都在默默地分类和归档。正因如此，那些考试前熬夜复习、彻夜不眠的学生，第二天往往觉得脑子昏昏沉沉，反而记不清前一晚背过的内容。研究还表明，睡得好的人学习效率更高，心情也更稳定。所以，与其牺牲睡眠去硬撑，不如保证充足的休息，让大脑安安静静地完成它的整理工作。

questions.q: 这段话主要想告诉我们什么？
options: 睡眠在记忆形成中起着重要作用
学生不应该参加任何考试
大脑在白天比夜里更活跃
休息会让人变得懒惰
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 수면 중 뇌가 기억을 정리·저장한다는 점과 '잠을 잘 자는 사람이 학습 효율이 높다'는 결론을 강조하므로, 수면이 기억 형성에 중요한 역할을 한다는 것이 핵심이다.

questions.q: 根据短文，考前熬夜复习的学生第二天记不清内容，最可能的原因是什么？
options: 他们复习的内容太简单
大脑没有得到睡眠去整理这些信息
他们白天没有认真学习
考试的题目太难了
questions.explanation: 본문은 뇌가 '잠든 동안' 정보를 정리하고 중요한 것을 기억한다고 했고, 밤새우면 뇌가 멍해져 기억하지 못한다고 했다. 따라서 수면이 없어 뇌가 정보를 정리하지 못한 것이 원인으로 추론된다.

questions.q: 文中把睡眠比作"认真的管理员"，这个比喻强调了睡眠的什么作用？
options: 让身体的肌肉得到放松
对信息进行分类和保存
帮助人们做有趣的梦
提醒人们按时起床
questions.explanation: '관리원' 비유 바로 앞뒤에서 뇌가 정보를 '다시 정리하고' '분류하고 보관한다'고 했으므로, 이 비유는 정보의 분류·저장 기능을 강조한다.

questions.q: 根据短文，睡得好的人有哪些表现？
options: 学习效率更高，心情更稳定
做事更快但容易生气
需要的睡眠时间越来越少
白天完全不会感到疲倦
questions.explanation: 본문에 '睡得好的人学习效率更高，心情也更稳定'이라고 직접 서술되어 있다.

questions.q: 作者最可能同意下面哪种做法？
options: 为了多学习，尽量减少睡眠
保证充足睡眠，让大脑完成整理
只在白天学习，晚上完全不复习

questions.explanation: 마지막 문장에서 '잠을 희생해 버티기보다 충분한 휴식을 보장해 뇌가 정리 작업을 하게 하라'고 했으므로 작가는 충분한 수면 보장에 동의한다.

gist.q: 这段话的中心意思是什么？
options: 睡眠帮助大脑整理和巩固记忆
熬夜是提高成绩的好办法

title: 城市里的共享单车
text: 近几年，五颜六色的共享单车出现在许多城市的街头巷尾，给人们的出行带来了不少方便。只要用手机扫一扫，就能随时骑走，到了目的地随手一停，再也不用担心找不到停车的地方。对于那些家离地铁站有一段距离的上班族来说，共享单车正好解决了"最后一公里"的难题。然而，方便的背后也藏着一些问题。有的人骑完车随意乱放，把单车堆在人行道上，挡住了行人的去路；有的车坏了却长期没人修理，渐渐变成了城市里的垃圾。要想让共享单车真正发挥作用，光靠企业是不够的，还需要每一位使用者自觉遵守规则。说到底，公共资源能不能用得长久，考验的正是大家共同的文明素质。

questions.q: 这段话主要谈论的是什么？

共享单车比地铁更适合上班族
城市里不应该再有共享单车
如何用手机扫码骑走单车
questions.explanation: 글은 공유자전거의 편리함과 그 이면의 문제를 함께 다루고, 마지막에 모두가 규칙을 지키고 함께 유지해야 한다고 결론짓는다.

questions.q: 根据短文，共享单车给上班族解决了什么难题？

questions.explanation: 본문에 집이 지하철역에서 떨어진 직장인에게 '最后一公里'(라스트 마일) 문제를 해결해 준다고 명시되어 있다.

options: 企业生产更多更便宜的单车

title: 蜜蜂的语言
text: 你也许想不到，小小的蜜蜂竟然拥有一套独特的"语言"。当一只蜜蜂在野外发现了一片开满鲜花的地方，它会飞回蜂巢，用一种奇特的舞蹈把消息告诉同伴。这种舞蹈被科学家称为"摇摆舞"。蜜蜂一边摆动身体，一边沿着一定的方向移动，舞蹈的方向和持续的时间，正好对应着花丛离蜂巢有多远、在哪个方位。同伴们就这样"看懂"了舞蹈，纷纷朝着正确的地方飞去采蜜。令人惊叹的是，这一切全靠身体动作完成，既不需要声音，也不需要文字。一只蜜蜂或许微不足道，可成千上万只蜜蜂通过这样的方式互相配合，就能让整个蜂群井井有条地运转。大自然的智慧，常常就藏在这些不起眼的小生命身上。

questions.q: 这段话主要介绍了什么？
options: 蜜蜂用舞蹈来传递信息的本领
蜜蜂如何采集花蜜酿成蜂蜜
蜂巢的内部是什么样子
鲜花为什么会吸引蜜蜂

questions.q: 根据短文，蜜蜂舞蹈的方向和时间长短能让同伴知道什么？
options: 花的颜色和香味

天气好不好

questions.q: 文中说这种舞蹈被科学家称为什么？

questions.explanation: 본문에 '这种舞蹈被科学家称为"摇摆舞"'라고 명시되어 있다.

questions.q: 作者说"一只蜜蜂或许微不足道"，紧接着想强调的是什么？
options: 单只蜜蜂没有任何用处

options: 大自然的智慧也体现在不起眼的小生命身上

title: 网上购物的烦恼
text: 如今，足不出户就能买到全世界的商品，网上购物早已成为许多人生活中不可缺少的一部分。然而，方便归方便，不少人也因此养成了乱花钱的习惯。购物网站上各式各样的促销活动层出不穷，再加上"只剩最后几件"这样的提示，常常让人一时冲动，把原本不需要的东西也加进了购物车。等快递送到，拆开包裹才发现，有的衣服尺码不合适，有的东西买回来根本用不上，只能堆在角落里落灰。专家提醒，在下单之前，最好先问问自己：这件东西我是真的需要，还是只是想要？养成理性消费的习惯，不仅能省下不少钱，也能让我们的生活变得更加简单清爽。学会克制，其实是一种难得的本领。

questions.q: 这段话主要想提醒人们注意什么？
options: 网上购物时要理性消费，避免冲动购买
网上购物比去商店更便宜
快递的速度越来越快

questions.explanation: 글은 온라인 쇼핑의 충동구매 문제를 지적하고 '이성적 소비 습관'을 기르라고 권하므로, 이성적 소비·충동구매 회피가 핵심이다.

questions.q: 根据短文，"只剩最后几件"这样的提示最可能起到什么作用？
options: 提醒顾客商品质量很好

帮助顾客比较价格
questions.explanation: 본문은 '只剩最后几件' 같은 안내가 '一时冲动'을 일으켜 필요 없는 물건까지 장바구니에 넣게 한다고 했으므로, 긴박감을 주어 충동구매를 유도한다.

questions.q: 文中提到冲动购物后可能出现什么结果？
options: 买到的东西都很实用

questions.q: 专家建议在下单前先问自己"是真的需要，还是只是想要"，这样做的目的是什么？
options: 为了买到更贵的商品

questions.q: 从最后一句"学会克制，其实是一种难得的本领"可以看出作者的什么态度？
options: 认为克制消费很困难，所以不值得提倡

title: 钙钛矿太阳能电池的稳定性难题
text: 钙钛矿材料因具备优异的光吸收能力与低廉的制备成本，被视为下一代太阳能电池的有力候选。短短十余年间，其实验室光电转换效率已逼近传统硅基电池，发展速度令业界惊叹。然而，真正阻碍其走向商业化的并非效率，而是稳定性。钙钛矿晶体结构对水分、氧气与高温都极为敏感，在户外持续光照下容易发生分解，导致器件性能在数月内迅速衰减。为攻克这一瓶颈，研究者尝试在材料表面引入致密的封装层，或通过调控化学组分来抑制离子迁移。值得注意的是，单纯延长寿命往往以牺牲部分效率为代价，因此如何在二者之间取得平衡，成为该领域当前最核心的课题。可以说，在激烈的竞争中，谁先解决稳定性问题，谁就可能主导这一新兴市场。

questions.q: 这段文字主要讨论的是什么？
options: 钙钛矿电池效率超越硅基电池的过程
制约钙钛矿电池商业化的稳定性问题及应对
太阳能在户外发电的经济效益
硅基太阳能电池的制造工艺改进
questions.explanation: 글 전체가 칼코게나이드(페로브스카이트) 전지의 상용화를 막는 핵심이 효율이 아니라 안정성임을 지적하고, 그 해결 시도와 과제를 다루므로 정답은 1번이다.

questions.q: 根据文章，钙钛矿晶体结构对下列哪些因素敏感？
options: 磁场、压力与噪声
水分、氧气与高温

questions.explanation: 본문에 '钙钛矿晶体结构对水分、氧气与高温都极为敏感'이라고 명시되어 있어 2번이 정답이다.

questions.q: 从文中可以推断，研究者面临的主要两难是什么？
options: 提高效率会同时降低制备成本

questions.explanation: '单纯延长寿命往往以牺牲部分效率为代价'에서 수명 연장과 효율 사이의 상충(딜레마)을 추론할 수 있으므로 2번이다.

questions.q: 文章提到研究者采用了哪种方法来提升稳定性？
options: 降低光照强度
引入致密的封装层或调控化学组分
增大电池面积
改用纯硅材料
questions.explanation: 본문은 표면에 치밀한 봉지층을 도입하거나 화학 조성을 조절해 이온 이동을 억제하는 방법을 든다. 따라서 2번이 정답이다.

questions.q: 作者认为谁可能主导这一新兴市场？
options: 最先大规模量产硅电池的企业
率先解决稳定性问题的一方
投入广告最多的厂商
效率最高但寿命最短的产品
questions.explanation: 마지막 문장 '谁先解决稳定性问题，谁就可能主导这一新兴市场'에서 안정성 문제를 먼저 해결한 쪽이 시장을 주도한다고 했으므로 2번이다.

gist.q: 全文的中心论点是？
options: 钙钛矿电池的效率已无可挑剔
稳定性而非效率是钙钛矿电池商业化的关键障碍
封装技术已彻底解决材料分解问题
硅基电池将很快被完全淘汰

scan.q: 文中指出真正阻碍商业化的不是效率，而是什么？

title: 城市热岛效应的成因与缓解
text: 在炎热的夏季，城市中心的气温往往明显高于周边乡村，这一现象被称为城市热岛效应。其成因是多方面的：密集的混凝土与沥青在白天大量吸收太阳辐射，到夜晚又缓慢释放热量；高楼林立阻碍了空气流通，使热量难以扩散；此外，空调、车辆与工业活动持续排放废热，进一步抬高了局部温度。热岛效应不仅加剧居民的不适感，还会推高制冷能耗，形成升温与耗能相互强化的恶性循环。为缓解这一问题，许多城市开始推广屋顶绿化与垂直绿化，利用植物的蒸腾作用降温；同时增设透水路面与水体，借助水分蒸发吸收热量。研究表明，仅靠扩大绿地面积难以根本扭转趋势，唯有将建筑设计、交通规划与生态布局统筹考虑，才能真正改善城市的热环境。

questions.q: 本文的核心内容是？
options: 夏季高温对农业的影响
城市热岛效应的成因及其缓解之道
空调技术的节能改进
乡村气候比城市更宜居的原因
questions.explanation: 글은 도시 열섬현상의 원인을 여러 측면에서 설명하고 완화 방법과 종합적 접근을 제시하므로 2번이 핵심이다.

questions.q: 根据文章，下列哪一项是热岛效应的成因之一？
options: 乡村植被过于茂密
混凝土与沥青白天吸热、夜晚缓慢放热

title: 固态电池为何被寄予厚望
text: 当前主流的锂离子电池普遍采用液态电解质，虽然技术成熟，却存在难以回避的安全隐患：在过度充电或机械损伤时，易燃的液态电解质可能引发起火甚至爆炸。固态电池以固体电解质取代液态电解质，从根本上降低了这种风险，因而被业界寄予厚望。除了安全性，固态电解质还允许使用能量密度更高的金属锂作为负极，理论上可显著延长续航里程。然而，理想与现实之间仍横亘着不少障碍。固体与固体之间的界面接触远不如液体与固体之间紧密，离子在界面处的传输阻力较大，导致电池在大电流下表现欠佳。此外，现有制造工艺成本高昂，难以满足大规模量产的需求。因此，尽管前景诱人，固态电池要真正取代液态电池，仍需在材料与工艺两条战线上同时取得突破。

固态电池的优势及其面临的现实障碍
金属锂的化学性质
电动汽车续航焦虑的成因

title: 深海采矿的资源诱惑与生态隐忧
text: 随着电动汽车与可再生能源的迅猛发展，对镍、钴、锰等关键金属的需求急剧攀升。陆地矿藏日渐稀缺，人们的目光转向了深海。在数千米的海床上，散布着大量富含这些金属的多金属结核，被视为潜力巨大的战略资源。然而，开采它们的代价远比想象中沉重。深海生态系统演化极为缓慢，许多生物依赖结核表面栖息繁衍，一旦采矿设备搅动海床，扬起的沉积物可能在大范围内长期悬浮，窒息底栖生物，而这种破坏几乎不可逆转。更令人担忧的是，深海环境至今研究甚少，科学界对潜在后果尚缺乏充分认识。正因如此，不少专家呼吁在弄清生态影响之前，应当暂缓大规模商业开采。这场围绕资源与环境的博弈，正考验着人类的远见与克制。

深海采矿的资源价值与生态风险之间的矛盾
陆地矿产的开采技术
多金属结核的化学组成

questions.q: 文中提到深海采矿令人担忧的一个重要原因是？

title: Constitutional Supercooling and the Onset of Cellular Breakdown
text:
The morphological stability of a planar solid-liquid interface advancing into an undercooled binary melt is governed less by the absolute magnitude of the imposed thermal gradient than by its ratio to the local growth velocity. As a solute-rejecting front advances, partitioning expels excess solute into the adjacent liquid, where, absent vigorous convection, it accumulates in a diffusion boundary layer whose characteristic thickness scales inversely with velocity. Because the equilibrium liquidus temperature depends on local composition, this enriched layer depresses the liquidus immediately ahead of the interface, generating a zone in which the actual temperature, though everywhere above the bulk solidus, falls below the local liquidus. Within this constitutionally supercooled region the melt is metastable: any incipient protrusion of the interface penetrates liquid that is already undercooled relative to its own composition and is thereby accelerated rather than thermally arrested. It is this positive feedback, and not capillarity, that the classical criterion identifies as the trigger for the loss of planarity.

Capillarity, however, is hardly inconsequential. The Gibbs-Thomson effect penalizes the curvature that any perturbation necessarily introduces, raising the equilibrium melting point of a convex tip and thus opposing its advance; concomitantly, the sharper the perturbation wavelength, the steeper the lateral solute gradients it must sustain. The selected morphology therefore emerges from a competition in which long-wavelength perturbations are favored by constitutional supercooling while short-wavelength ones are suppressed by surface energy, leaving an intermediate band of marginally unstable wavelengths from which the cellular spacing is ultimately selected. That the constitutional criterion frequently predicts breakdown somewhat prematurely, relative to what dynamical stability analysis yields, is unsurprising: the former neglects the stabilizing contribution of interfacial energy altogether. Yet its enduring utility lies precisely in its parsimony. By collapsing the breakdown condition onto a single dimensionless grouping of gradient, velocity, and the imposed compositional range, it furnishes a first approximation that, while quantitatively imperfect, remains qualitatively faithful across an extraordinary range of alloy systems and processing rates.

questions.q: Which statement best captures the central argument of the passage?
options: Constitutional supercooling and capillarity are competing effects whose interplay, not either alone, determines whether and how a planar interface breaks down.
Surface energy is the dominant factor governing interface stability, and the constitutional criterion is largely obsolete.
Convection in the melt is the primary determinant of the diffusion boundary layer thickness.
The absolute thermal gradient is the single most reliable predictor of cellular spacing.
questions.explanation: 지문 전체는 헌법적 과냉(불안정화)과 모세관 효과(안정화)의 경쟁으로 형태가 결정된다고 논증한다. 1번이 이 핵심을 포착한다. 2번은 헌법적 기준을 쓸모없다고 하지만 지문은 그 유용성을 강조하므로 오답. 3번은 부수적 언급일 뿐이고, 4번은 '절대 크기보다 비율'이라는 첫 문장과 모순된다.

questions.q: It can be inferred that the classical constitutional criterion tends to predict interface breakdown at conditions where dynamical stability analysis would still predict a stable planar front because:
options: it overestimates the rate of solute rejection at the interface
it omits the stabilizing penalty that surface energy imposes on curved perturbations
it assumes vigorous convection that flattens the boundary layer
it treats the thermal gradient as more important than growth velocity
questions.explanation: 지문은 헌법적 기준이 '계면 에너지의 안정화 기여를 전적으로 무시(neglects the stabilizing contribution of interfacial energy altogether)'하므로 다소 이르게 붕괴를 예측한다고 명시한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 1번(it omits the stabilizing penalty...). 나머지는 지문이 제시한 이유와 무관하거나 반대된다.

questions.q: Based on the passage, why are very short-wavelength perturbations of the interface suppressed?
options: Because they accumulate solute more slowly than long-wavelength perturbations
Because the diffusion boundary layer cannot form around them
Because the curvature they introduce raises the tip's equilibrium melting point and demands steeper lateral solute gradients
Because the bulk solidus temperature rises locally around sharp tips
questions.explanation: 지문은 Gibbs-Thomson 효과가 곡률에 페널티를 주어 볼록한 팁의 평형 융점을 높이고, 파장이 날카로울수록 더 가파른 측면 용질 구배를 유지해야 한다고 설명한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 2번. 4번의 'solidus'는 지문에서 융점(liquidus/melting point)과 구별되며 곡률과 연결되지 않았다.

questions.q: According to the passage, the characteristic thickness of the diffusion boundary layer:
options: increases in proportion to growth velocity
scales inversely with growth velocity
is independent of growth velocity
is determined solely by the thermal gradient
questions.explanation: 지문은 경계층 두께가 속도에 반비례한다(scales inversely with velocity)고 명시한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 1번이고 나머지는 직접 모순된다.

questions.q: The author's attitude toward the classical constitutional criterion is best described as:
options: dismissive of its predictions as fundamentally misleading
appreciative of its parsimony despite acknowledged quantitative imperfection
convinced it should replace dynamical stability analysis
skeptical that it applies to more than a narrow class of alloys
questions.explanation: 마지막 문단에서 저자는 기준이 정량적으로 불완전하지만 그 절약성(parsimony)과 광범위한 적용성 때문에 지속적 유용성을 가진다고 평가한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 1번. 4번은 'extraordinary range of alloy systems'와 모순된다.

gist.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: How the competition between constitutional supercooling and capillarity governs the morphological stability of a solidification front
Why convection must be eliminated to achieve planar solidification
A proof that the Gibbs-Thomson effect alone determines cellular spacing
The measurement of solidus temperatures in binary alloys

scan.q: What single grouping does the constitutional criterion collapse the breakdown condition onto, according to the passage?
scan.answer: a single dimensionless grouping of gradient, velocity, and the imposed compositional range

title: Thermal Cycling and Residual Stress in Laser Powder-Bed Fusion
text:
In laser powder-bed fusion, the part is not so much built as repeatedly reconstituted: each newly deposited layer subjects the subjacent material to a transient thermal excursion whose peak may exceed the melting point and whose subsequent quench proceeds at rates that, in some alloys, approach the limits of conventional measurement. The residual stress field that survives this history is, in consequence, neither uniform nor straightforwardly attributable to a single mechanism. The dominant contribution is commonly framed through the temperature-gradient mechanism, wherein the steep gradient established during heating drives the irradiated surface to expand against the constraint of cooler, mechanically stiffer material beneath. Constrained from expanding freely, the hot layer yields in compression; upon cooling it would contract, but the surrounding material now resists, leaving the once-compressed region locked in tension and bequeathing a self-equilibrating stress distribution to the consolidated solid.

What complicates any tidy account is that this mechanism operates not once but cyclically, and each pass partially relaxes, redistributes, or even reverses the stresses imprinted by its predecessors through localized re-austenitization, tempering, or stress relief, depending on the proximity of the deposited track and the alloy's transformation kinetics. The accumulated state is thus path-dependent in a strong sense: nominally identical geometries produced under differing scan strategies can exhibit markedly divergent stress topographies, a sensitivity that frustrates attempts to predict distortion from steady-state thermal arguments alone. It would be incautious, therefore, to infer the final stress field from peak temperatures in isolation; the sequence and spatial register of thermal events matter at least as much as their magnitude. Mitigation strategies that exploit this fact—island or checkerboard scanning that shortens the characteristic length over which gradients integrate, or substrate preheating that diminishes the gradient outright—do not so much eliminate residual stress as renegotiate its distribution, trading peak magnitudes against the volume over which they are dispersed. That such palliatives remain partial is a reminder that the stress is, in the end, an inheritance of the thermal history rather than an incidental defect.

questions.q: Which statement best expresses the main point of the passage?
options: Residual stress in laser powder-bed fusion is a path-dependent inheritance of cyclic thermal history that mitigation can redistribute but not eliminate.
Residual stress arises entirely from the peak temperature reached during the first deposited layer.
Checkerboard scanning fully eliminates residual stress by removing thermal gradients.
Residual stress is an incidental defect that can be removed by post-processing alone.
questions.explanation: 지문은 잔류응력이 주기적 열이력의 경로의존적 유산이며 완화 전략이 분포를 재협상할 뿐 제거하지 못한다고 논증한다. 답은 인덱스 0번. 2번은 'cyclically'와 모순, 3번은 '재협상할 뿐 제거 못함'과 모순, 4번은 'incidental defect rather than'이라는 마지막 문장과 정반대다.

questions.q: Why does the author caution against inferring the final stress field from peak temperatures alone?
options: Because peak temperatures cannot be measured accurately in any alloy
Because the sequence and spatial arrangement of thermal events matter at least as much as their magnitude
Because the melting point is irrelevant to stress formation
Because residual stress depends only on the substrate preheating temperature
questions.explanation: 지문은 열 사건의 순서와 공간적 배치(sequence and spatial register)가 그 크기만큼 중요하다고 명시하며, 따라서 피크 온도만으로 추론하는 것은 경솔하다고 본다. 답은 인덱스 1번.

questions.q: According to the temperature-gradient mechanism as described, the region that was compressed during heating ends up in tension because:
options: it melts a second time during cooling
the surrounding cooler material resists its contraction upon cooling
it never actually yields during the heating phase
the substrate preheating raises its yield strength
questions.explanation: 지문은 가열 중 압축으로 항복한 영역이 냉각 시 수축하려 하나 주변 재료가 저항하여 인장 상태로 고정된다고 설명한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 1번.

questions.q: It can be inferred that two parts with identical geometry but different scan strategies may show different residual stress because:
options: the alloy composition changes with scan strategy
the cyclic redistribution of stress is path-dependent
steady-state thermal analysis fully determines the outcome
peak temperatures are always identical regardless of strategy
questions.explanation: 지문은 누적 상태가 강한 의미에서 경로의존적이어서 동일 형상이라도 스캔 전략에 따라 응력 분포가 크게 달라진다고 명시한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 1번. 3번은 정상상태 논증으로는 예측이 좌절된다는 서술과 모순된다.

questions.q: How does the passage characterize the effect of island/checkerboard scanning and substrate preheating?
options: They eliminate residual stress completely
They renegotiate the stress distribution, trading peak magnitude against dispersed volume
They increase the peak stress to relieve distortion
They have no measurable effect on the stress field
questions.explanation: 지문은 이들 전략이 응력을 제거하기보다 분포를 재협상하며 피크 크기와 분산 부피를 맞바꾼다고 설명한다. 따라서 답은 인덱스 1번.

gist.q: What is the passage primarily concerned with?
options: Explaining how residual stress in laser powder-bed fusion originates from and is shaped by cyclic thermal history
